claim: Agent teams in Claude Code feature a team lead (project manager) plus specialized agents sharing a task list where teammates can communicate with each other, unlike subagents which work independently
actionable: Use agent teams when you need parallel work with interdependencies and inter-agent communication, use subagents when tasks are sequential or independent
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Enable agent teams in Claude Code by adding CLAUDE_ENABLE_AGENT_TEAMS=true to .claude/settings.local.json as an experimental feature
actionable: Configure agent teams in your Claude Code project by adding the required environment variable to your project settings
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Prompt agent teams using natural language with pattern: Create a team of X agents using X model, listing each agent's role and responsibilities with clear communication flow
actionable: When invoking agent teams, specify goal, team size, model, agent roles, and explicitly define which agents message which teammates
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Each agent team member should own specific files and deliverables to prevent overwriting and conflicts; they should communicate directly rather than through the main session
actionable: Assign explicit file ownership to each team member and design prompts to encourage direct peer-to-peer communication instead of funneling through the orchestrator
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Agent teams inherit permissions from the main session including access to all files, MCP servers, and skills automatically
actionable: Understand that all teammates have the same permissions and access as the main Claude Code session when you enable permissions in your project
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Use tmux split-pane view when running agent teams to visually observe individual agent thinking and work in parallel, enabling better debugging and intervention
actionable: Run Claude Code agent teams in tmux terminal to see split-pane views showing real-time agent activity instead of using the VS Code extension
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Agent teams work best for complex multi-area projects requiring parallel work, inter-agent communication, and high quality; avoid teams for sequential tasks better suited to subagents
actionable: Reserve agent teams for projects with multiple specialized domains needing parallel work and QA loops; use subagents for sequential task pipelines
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Agent teams are more expensive and slower than subagents but produce higher quality; limit to 2-5 agents max to manage costs and complexity
actionable: When using agent teams, limit to 3-5 specialized agents and avoid creating swarms of 10+ agents due to exponential cost and coordination overhead
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: Plan approval mode allows agent teammates to create plans that must be approved before execution, improving reliability for high-stakes tasks
actionable: Enable plan approval mode in agent teams to have the orchestrator or a specialized teammate review and approve plans before execution
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People

claim: QA agents in teams can find critical issues and send work back to implementation agents for rework, creating effective quality gates
actionable: Include a QA/critic agent in your team to validate other agents' work and trigger revision loops when quality falls short
source_title: How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People
